findings.description: Windows Firewall is DISABLED on 1 profile(s): Public.
findings.recommendation: Enable the Windows Firewall on all profiles via: Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -Enabled True

findings.description: SMBv1 is ENABLED. This legacy protocol is exploited by EternalBlue (MS17-010) and ransomware such as WannaCry.
Name-poisoning attack surface is EXPOSED: LLMNR is enabled; NetBIOS over TCP/IP is enabled on one or more adapters.
findings.recommendation: Disable LLMNR via GPO: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Network → DNS Client → Turn Off Multicast Name Resolution = Enabled. Disable NetBIOS via NIC settings or DHCP option 001.

findings.description: Windows Defender identifies issues: Antivirus signatures are 31 day(s) old (>7 days).
findings.recommendation: Enable Defender: Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $false. Update signatures: Update-MpSignature.

findings.description: Deprecated protocols enabled: TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1. These are vulnerable to POODLE, BEAST, DROWN, and downgrade attacks.
findings.recommendation: Disable deprecated protocols via SCHANNEL registry keys. Use IIS Crypto or configure SCHANNEL manually.
findings.raw_output:
SSL 2.0: 0
SSL 3.0: 0
TLS 1.0: NOT_SET
TLS 1.1: NOT_SET

findings.description: Password policy weaknesses detected: Minimum password length is 7 (should be ≥12); Account lockout threshold is 0 (accounts never locked out).
findings.recommendation: Configure via Group Policy: Computer Configuration → Windows Settings → Security Settings → Account Policies → Password Policy. Set min length ≥12, max age ≤90 days, enable complexity, set lockout threshold ≤5.
findings.raw_output:
Force user logoff how long after time expires?:       Never
Minimum password age (days):                         0
Maximum password age (days):                         42
Minimum password length:                             7
Length of password history maintained:               0
Lockout threshold:                                   Never

findings.description: RDP does NOT require Network Level Authentication (NLA). This exposes the login screen to unauthenticated users and is exploited by BlueKeep (CVE-2019-0708).
findings.recommendation: Enable NLA: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp' -Name UserAuthentication -Value 1 or via System Properties → Remote → Require NLA.

findings.description: 12 update(s) pending, including 3 CRITICAL patch(es). Unpatched systems are vulnerable to known exploits.
findings.recommendation: Apply all pending critical updates immediately via Windows Update.

findings.description: Administrator account issues: 5 accounts in Administrators group (expected ≤3); Built-in Administrator account is enabled. Current members: Administrator, CORP\Domain Admins, CORP\svc-admin, john.smith, helpdesk.
findings.recommendation: Disable built-in Administrator: Disable-LocalUser -Name Administrator. Remove unnecessary accounts from Administrators group. Use LAPS for local admin management.

findings.description: Multiple privileged groups have members: Backup Operators has 2 member(s); Remote Desktop Users has 1 member(s); Remote Management Users has 1 member(s).
findings.recommendation: Review and remove unnecessary memberships. Apply the principle of least privilege. Use Just-In-Time (JIT) access for privileged roles.
findings.raw_output:
Backup Operators: CORP\backup-svc,CORP\old-backup
Remote Desktop Users: CORP\rd-users
Remote Management Users: CORP\winrm-svc
Power Users: _EMPTY_

findings.description: Event log configuration issues: Security log is only 20 MB (min recommended: 64 MB); System log is only 20 MB (min recommended: 64 MB).
findings.recommendation: Configure via GPO: Computer Configuration → Windows Settings → Security Settings → Event Log. Set Security log to ≥128 MB with RetentionDays=0 (overwrite as needed).

findings.description: LSASS is vulnerable to credential dumping: LSASS Protected Process Light (RunAsPPL) is not enabled; Credential Guard is not enabled. Tools like Mimikatz can extract plaintext credentials.
findings.recommendation: Enable RunAsPPL: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa' -Name RunAsPPL -Value 1. Enable Credential Guard via GPO or Device Guard.

findings.description: Weak cipher suites detected: RC4, 3DES. These enable downgrade attacks and break-in via weak encryption.
findings.recommendation: Disable weak ciphers via SCHANNEL registry. Use IIS Crypto tool or configure via New-ItemProperty.
findings.raw_output:
RC4: NOT_SET
DES: 0
3DES: NOT_SET
NULL: 0
EXPORT: 0

findings.description: 2 file share(s) with overly permissive access: 'Public' grants Full to Everyone; 'Shared' grants Change to Authenticated Users.
findings.recommendation: Remove 'Everyone' and 'Authenticated Users' from share ACLs. Grant access only to specific groups (principle of least privilege).

findings.description: 2 EOL/outdated application(s) detected: Java 8 Update 201, Adobe Flash Player 32.
findings.recommendation: Uninstall or upgrade EOL applications. EOL software no longer receives security patches.
